message:
fix(config): guard memLimitPrefix against missing systemd user session

memLimitPrefix() previously checked only runtime.GOOS == "linux" before
emitting a systemd-run --user prefix. In Docker containers, devcontainers,
WSL1, and other environments without a systemd user session, this causes
agent launch to fail because systemd-run --user cannot connect to the
user session bus.

Add a sync.OnceValue guard that probes for the per-user systemd private
socket (/run/user/<uid>/systemd/private) before enabling the prefix.
When the socket is absent, memLimitPrefix() returns an empty string,
allowing agents to launch without cgroup memory limiting.

Fixes #85

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
